title: Reading - Part 1
instructions: For each question, choose the correct answer.

questions.heading: School Trip Bags
questions.text: Students going on the castle trip should bring one small bag only. Large bags cannot be taken inside the castle and there is nowhere safe to leave them on the coach.
questions.question: What does the notice tell students?

options.C: They should avoid bringing too much luggage.
options.A: They must leave bags on the coach.
options.B: They cannot take anything into the castle.

questions.explanation: Students are told to bring one small bag because large bags are unsuitable.

questions.heading: Message from Ava to Dylan
questions.text: Dylan, I’ve found the recipe book we need, but the library copy is for reference only. I’ll take photos of the pages if that’s allowed, so we can use them at home.
questions.question: Why is Ava texting Dylan?

options.A: To explain how they might use the book.
options.B: To ask him to return a library book.
options.C: To say the recipe book is missing.

questions.explanation: The book cannot be borrowed, so Ava suggests taking photos if possible.

questions.heading: Weekend Pottery Class
questions.text: Make a small bowl in our pottery class this Saturday. Your bowl will need to dry before it is painted, so you must return the following week to finish it.
questions.question: What should people understand?

options.B: The activity cannot be completed in one session.
options.A: They need to bring a bowl from home.
options.C: Painting will happen before the bowl dries.

questions.explanation: Participants must return the following week to finish their bowl.

questions.heading: From: Mr Allen / Subject: Reading homework
questions.text: Several students chose books that are too difficult for the review task. If you cannot explain the main story easily, choose another book before Monday and tell me your new title.
questions.question: What does Mr Allen advise?

options.C: Change books if the story is unclear.
options.A: Review a difficult book before Monday.
options.B: Explain every title to the class.

questions.explanation: Students should choose another book if they cannot explain the story easily.

questions.heading: School Festival Volunteers
questions.text: We need volunteers to show visitors around the school festival. You will work in pairs, and maps will be provided. Please come to a short meeting on Wednesday to choose your time.
questions.question: What must volunteers do first?

options.A: Attend a meeting to arrange their work.
options.B: Make maps for visitors before Wednesday.
options.C: Show visitors around without a partner.

questions.explanation: Volunteers need to attend a meeting to choose their time.
